productOverview.name: Government PDF-to-Digital Form Builder
productOverview.scope: Hackathon MVP — 5 sprints, demo-ready end-to-end flow
productOverview.elevatorPitch: A reusable platform that ingests government PDF forms, extracts a draft field structure, allows back-office editors to review and correct it, and publishes a schema definition that a consuming application renders as an accessible digital form journey.
targetUsers: Applicant: citizen completing a digital form journey
Form editor: reviews extracted draft, edits field labels, types, and required flags
productOverview.mockAuth: Hardcoded demo-editor user — no login screen

coreFeatures.title: PDF Upload & Ingestion
coreFeatures.description: Back-office users upload a PDF form. The ingestion service stores the file and triggers extraction.
acceptanceCriteria: User can upload a PDF via a GOV.UK-styled file-upload component
Uploaded file is stored and associated with a unique form ID and timestamp
Unsupported file types produce a clear, inline error message
Upload page uses govuk-frontend components for baseline WCAG compliance
a11yRequirements: File upload uses govuk-frontend file-upload component with correct label association
Error messages use govuk-frontend error-summary and inline error patterns

coreFeatures.title: Automated Field Extraction
coreFeatures.description: After upload, the extraction engine analyses the PDF and produces a draft form schema containing detected field labels and inferred types.
acceptanceCriteria: Extraction runs automatically after successful upload
Draft schema produced containing: id, label, type, required, order per field
Supported field types: text, textarea, date, number, radio, checkbox, select
Extraction works reliably on at least 1 curated digitally generated test PDF
a11yRequirements: Status feedback uses govuk-frontend notification banner pattern

coreFeatures.title: Form Builder UI – Field Editor (Trimmed)
coreFeatures.description: An editor can view the extracted draft, correct field labels and types, toggle required, and add/remove fields.
acceptanceCriteria: Editor can rename field labels and change field types (all 7 types selectable)
Editor can toggle the required flag per field
Editor can add new fields and delete existing fields
All form controls use govuk-frontend components with associated labels
Editor UI is keyboard-operable
a11yRequirements: All inputs have visible, programmatically associated labels (WCAG 1.3.1)
Focus management maintained when adding/removing fields

coreFeatures.title: Schema Save & Publish (Simplified)
coreFeatures.description: The editor can save the current schema and publish it, making it available to the renderer via the API.
acceptanceCriteria: Save persists the schema to the in-memory store with a version number
Publish button marks the schema as published and sets it as the active version
Version number increments on each save
Schema is stored as canonical JSON conforming to the Form Schema specification

coreFeatures.title: Runtime API – Schema Serving (Simplified)
coreFeatures.description: A single REST endpoint serves the active published schema for a form.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/forms/:formId/schema returns the active published schema as JSON
404 returned for unknown formId
Response includes metadata: formId, version, publishedAt

coreFeatures.title: Dynamic Form Renderer (Simplified)
coreFeatures.description: A reference page fetches a published form schema and renders a single-page GOV.UK-styled form with client-side required validation.
acceptanceCriteria: Renderer fetches schema from Runtime API on load
Renders correct govuk-frontend field components for each field type
Client-side required validation shows GOV.UK error summary on submit
Submission produces a structured JSON payload
Completion page uses GOV.UK panel component with reference number
Full keyboard journey from start to submission works without mouse
a11yRequirements: All field components use govuk-frontend: input, textarea, radios, checkboxes, date-input, select
Error summary links focus to the relevant field on activation (WCAG 2.4.3)

removedFeatures.title: Review & Publish Workflow
removedFeatures.reason: Enterprise approval workflow — zero demo value. Publish button in editor replaces it.

removedFeatures.title: Role-Based Access Control & Audit Trail
removedFeatures.reason: Auth, RBAC, audit log are enterprise concerns. Hardcoded mock user for hackathon.

uiDirection.styleNotes: All UI must use the GOV.UK Design System exclusively. No custom CSS frameworks. govuk-frontend components provide baseline WCAG 2.2 AA compliance.
uiDirection.wcagGuidance: Baseline WCAG 2.2 AA via govuk-frontend components. Key criteria: 1.3.1, 1.4.3, 2.1.1, 2.4.2, 2.4.3, 2.4.6, 3.3.1, 3.3.2, 4.1.2.

sprintBreakdown.title: PDF Upload & Extraction
sprintBreakdown.description: Express backend setup, in-memory store, file upload endpoint, PDF text extraction (pdf-parse), produce draft schema JSON. GOV.UK styled upload page.

sprintBreakdown.title: Form Builder UI
sprintBreakdown.description: React + Vite frontend. Display extracted fields in a list. Edit label, type, required flag. Add/remove fields. All using govuk-frontend components.

sprintBreakdown.title: Save, Publish & Serve
sprintBreakdown.description: Persist edited schema to store. Publish button marks it active. GET /api/forms/:formId/schema serves published schema. Version number increments on save.

sprintBreakdown.title: Dynamic Form Renderer
sprintBreakdown.description: Separate page/route fetches published schema. Renders single-page GOV.UK form with correct field components. Client-side required validation. Submit shows confirmation panel.

sprintBreakdown.title: E2E Integration & Demo Polish
sprintBreakdown.description: Wire full flow with 1 curated PDF. Fix styling issues. Ensure happy path works smoothly end-to-end. Basic accessibility check (correct labels, keyboard nav, focus management).
